summary: Iteration 31 - Comprehensive testing of ALL new NOC features. Backend: 36/36 tests passed (100%). Frontend: All pages and widgets verified working. Features tested: PDF Reports, Device Inventory, Incident Management, Port/Service Monitoring, Public Dashboard, Notification Templates, Escalation Rules, Dashboard Widgets (SLA, Changes, Heatmap, Latency).

issues: Minor: Chart width warning in console when page first loads (recharts library issue, not blocking)

pdf_reports: GET /api/reports/list - Lists clients available for reports
device_inventory: GET /api/inventory/{client_id} - Returns full device inventory with stats
Search filter working (by IP, name, MAC)
Type filter working (switch, firewall, server, ap)
Sorting working (by IP, name, type, etc.)
incident_management: PATCH /api/incidents/{id} - Updates priority (low -> medium -> high -> critical)
GET /api/incidents/stats/summary - Returns stats by status and priority
DELETE /api/incidents/{id} - Deletes incident and verifies removal
public_dashboard: Shows client name, SLA (95.39%), devices (8/10 online), alerts (1 active)
frontend_pages: Dashboard page loads with all 4 widgets (SLA, Changes, Heatmap, Latency)
Inventory page loads with table, filters, stats
Incidents page loads with create button, stats, detail panel
Port Monitor page loads with check all, add service buttons
Reports page loads with client select, period select, generate button
Public Dashboard loads without auth, shows SLA/devices/alerts
sidebar_navigation: Monitoraggio group with Dashboard, Alert, Stato Rete, Dispositivi, Inventario, Monitor Servizi
Operazioni group with Incidenti, Report PDF
All nav items clickable and navigate correctly

critical_code_review_comments: All new routes properly registered in server.py (lines 133-161)
Public dashboard correctly skips authentication for GET /api/public/dashboard/{token}
Incident timeline properly tracks all changes with user and timestamp
PDF generation uses reportlab with proper styling and tables
Port monitor uses asyncio for TCP connection checks with timeout
Notification test endpoint is MOCKED (returns success stub without actual sending)
seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Used existing client 86BIT_Office with 10 devices.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 31 verified ALL new PRTG/CloudFire-inspired features: 1) PDF Reports generate valid PDFs with SLA, devices, alerts, changes. 2) Device Inventory shows all 10 devices with filters/search/sorting. 3) Incident Management has full CRUD with timeline tracking. 4) Port Monitor can add/check/remove TCP services. 5) Public Dashboard at /public/15ec0bf5b908af5d is accessible without auth and shows SLA (95.39%), devices (8/10 online), alerts. 6) Notification Templates API has CRUD (actual sending is MOCKED). 7) Dashboard widgets (SLA Gauge, Change Timeline, Heatmap, Latency) all working.

mocked_apis.notification_test: POST /api/notifications/test returns success stub without actual sending (requires external API keys for SendGrid, Twilio, Firebase)

pdf_reports.output: Valid PDF with SLA, devices, alerts, changes

device_inventory.features: Search, type filter, status filter, sorting

incident_management.features: Create, update status/priority, add notes, timeline, delete

port_monitor.features: Add service, check TCP ports, remove service

public_dashboard.features: No auth required, shows SLA/devices/alerts, auto-refresh
